# Contractors License Board
The Board licenses Certified Public Contractor, enforces standards, renews licenses, and conducts disciplinary actions ensuring ethical accounting practices
Licensing Made Simple

  * [Check the Status of Your Application](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/check-the-status-of-your-application/)
  * Inactivate or Reactivate Your License
  * Request an Address or Name Change or Duplicate License (pocket ID/wall certificate)
  * Maintain Entity-RME Requirements
  * [Apply for Additional Classification](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/contractor/apply-for-additional-classification/)
  * [Apply for Other Changes, (Status, Conversion, RME)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/contractor/apply-for-other-changes-status-conversion-rme/)
  * Inactivate or Reactivate Your License
  * Request an Address or Name Change or Duplicate License (pocket ID/wall certificate)
  * Request a License Verification
  * Manage Your License Online With Your MyPVL LICENSEE DASHBOARD


License Continuation Info
## Renewal Information
(Renew by September 30 of every even-numbered year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of October 1, 2026 to September 30, 2028
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
August 10, 2026 to September 30, 2026
  * ##### Renewal Hardcopy Form Mailout:
August 7, 2026
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date:
September 30, 2026


Renew Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Entity – active status -
$338.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Sole Proprietor – active status - 
$338.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) RME – active status - 
$208.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) ALL – inactive status - 
$160.00


  * All licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by September 30, every even-numbered year.


  * Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.

**Additional Renewal Requirements**  
---  
|  **TAX CLEARANCE REQUIREMENTS**  
for Active Contractor Entity and Sole Proprietors
  * Must have current and valid tax clearance with DOTAX
  * Tax clearance must be within 6 months of renewing license
  * Tax clearance DOTAX name must match the contractor’s PVL license name

  
---  
**If DOTAX Name Does Not Match PVL Licensee Name** If you want to update your tax clearance name with the Hawaii Department of Taxation: 1. Sign into Hawaii Tax Online at: <https://hitax.hawaii.gov/> 2. Select More… tab, Registration tile, select Manage Names & DBAs. 3. Navigate to the tax account, select the Name you would like to update. 4. Complete the request and select Submit.” OR Please contact the Department of Taxation at 808-587-4242 between the hours of 7:45 AM – 4:00 PM (Hawaii Standard Time), Monday to Friday, except state holidays to obtain a valid tax clearance that matches your PVL license name exactly.  
Firms and Sole Proprietors
RMEs
Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information (Restore within 60 days after license expiration)
Forfeited licenses may be restored within 60 days and subject to meeting restoration requirements. Failure to restore a forfeited license within 60 days after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.
  * ##### How to Request Hardcopy Restoration Form
[Learn More ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/request-hardcopy-restoration-form/)


#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Entity – active status -
$388.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Sole Proprietor – active status - 
$388.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) RME – active status - 
$258.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) ALL – inactive status - 
$210.00

##  Contractors License Board Meeting Schedule 
Meeting agendas are posted on the State Calendar site at least six calendar days before the meeting.
Contractor Applicant List
The current applicant list will be posted not less than fourteen (14) days prior to each Board meeting date and will be removed one (1) day after the scheduled Board meeting is held.
  * [February Applicant List](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/February-2026-Applicant-List.pdf)

State of Hawaii Department
# Supporting Commerce & Consumers in Hawai‘i
How can we help you today?
Search for service
Clear Search 
Popular Searches:
License lookup
Business Registration
Medical License
Business name search
Guidance. Licensing. Compliance.
## Our Services
We have organized our services to create a clear path for consumers, businesses, and institutions
### For the Public   
and Consumers
Consumer advocacy hub for ensuring a fair, and transparent marketplace
!a woman using a touch screen laptop
  * Verify a Professional's License
  * Search Disciplinary Actions and Judgments
  * Report Unlicensed Activity in the PV&L areas
  * Hawai‘i Lemon Law for Vehicles


View all Services For the Public   
and Consumers 
### For Businesses, Professionals and Institutions
The official starting point for business registration and professional licensing
!customer making a credit card payment at the counter
  * [Apply for or Renew a Professional License](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl)
  * [Register a New Business Entity](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/BizEx/home.eb)
  * File an Annual Report
  * [Find Your Board’s Rules and Requirements](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards-and-programs/)


View all Services For Businesses, Professionals and Institutions 
### For Institutions
Compliance guidance and support for Hawaii's regulated industries
!building with trees at the University of Hawaii
  * Guidance for Financial Institutions
  * [Information for Insurers (General and Captive)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/ins/)
  * Authorization for Post-Secondary Schools
  * [Requirements for Time Share](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/programs/timeshare/)


View all Resources For Institutions 
Need Help? Start Here
## Consumer Complaints
Report unfair business practices. Choose a category below to get started
 File a Complaint 
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/need-help-img.webp)
Keep Up to Date
## News and Updates
Latest updates on the policies, people, and actions that are shaping our state's marketplace
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/commissioner-of-securities-orders-cease-and-desist-against-teapresso-bar-llc/)
October 12, 2025
### Release: Commissioner of Securities Orders Cease and Desist Against Teapresso Bar LLC

October 12, 2025

 ! 
July 12, 2025
### Release: DCCA Disciplinary Actions (Through September 2025)
 ! 
June 15, 2025
### Release: Fourth Quarter Hawaiʻi Annual Business Reports Due

Our mission at the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs is to protect Hawaii’s consumers and service its business community with respect and fairness to the interests of both.

Grow and Succeed
# For Businesses and Professionals
Everything you need to operate your business, manage your license, and ensure compliance in Hawaii.
!two people looking at products on a tablet in a retail store
Getting Started
## Start a Business
The essential steps to register your company, secure licenses, and protect your brand identity.
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-10.svg)
### Register a Business
The formal beginning for your new company or LLC in Hawaii
[ Go to Register a Business  ](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/BizEx/home.eb)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-11.svg)
### Apply for a license online
Obtain the professional or vocational license required for specific industry
[ Go to Apply for a license online  ](https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-12.svg)
### Register a Trade Name or Trademark
Officially register your business trade name, trademark, or service mark
[ Go to Register a Trade Name or Trademark  ](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/BizEx/home.eb)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-13.svg)
### Purchase a Business Registration Document
Request a Certificate of Good Standing or official certified copies of your filings
[ Go to Purchase a Business Registration Document  ](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/documents/search)
Stay Current
## Manage Your  
License
MyPVL is your 24/7 dashboard for all licensee services and information. Use your free myHawaii account for secure, single sign-on access to renew, update records, and more.
Go to MyPVL Portal
!close up of hands typing on a silver laptop keyboard
!view of a city scape with high rise buildings
Stay Compliant
## Navigate Compliance
Stay current with all state requirements to keep your business or professional license in good standing. Fulfill annual reporting duties, verify your compliance status, and review continuing education mandates.
  * Hawaii Compliance Express 
  * File Annual Reports
  * [Continuing Education Information](https://myce.dcca.hawaii.gov/)


Key Sectors
## The Regulated   
Industries
What Industries, professionals, and tradespersons fall within RICO’s enforcement authority?
To protect public safety, a license is required in many professions, assuring state standards are met. Explore the complete list or review our dedicated resources for specific fields.
View Full List
!Lady Justice statue with scales and sword.
Major Entities
## For Institutions
The official source for industry-specific directives, licensing requirements, and compliance information.
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-25.svg)
### Banks
Information on state-regulated banks, consumer resources, and filings
 Go to Banks  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-4.svg)
### Insurance
Agent licensing, consumer complaints, and insurer compliance information
 Go to Insurance  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-6.svg)
### Securities
Investor protection, entity registration, and resources to avoid fraud
 Go to Securities  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-2.svg)
### Utilities and Cable Companies
Information on providers and help with service or billing
 Go to Utilities and Cable Companies  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-26.svg)
### Educational Institutions
List of licensed schools and resources for students/administrators
 Go to Educational Institutions  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-27.svg)
### Real Estate
Guidance on licensing, condo governance, licensee checks, consumer resources, and complaints
 Go to Real Estate

How to Reach Us
# Contact Us
Find contact information for all DCCA Hawaii divisions. Get addresses, phone numbers, and office hours for inquiries, support, or to file a complaint
!A group of diverse professionals sitting around a conference table with laptops in a bright modern office with a city view

Resources at Your Fingertips
## Helpful Links and Tools
Essential tools and resources to file a complaint, find business and consumer resources, or make a public records request
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-15.svg)
### File a Complaint
If you can’t resolve an issue directly with a professional or business, file a complaint with DCCA. Visit our Complaints page for instructions.
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-16.svg)
### Resources
Look up businesses, licensees, and individuals. Businesses can register, renew licenses, and purchase certificates and bulk data. For details, visit our Resources
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-17.svg)
### Consumer Resource Center
Get help hiring licensed contractors, reporting unlicensed activity, and filing complaints against professionals. Call 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)),press option 7
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-18.svg)
### Open Records Request
The [Uniform Information Practices Act](https://oip.hawaii.gov/laws-rules-opinions/uipa/) (Modified), chapter 92F, Hawaii Revised Statutes (“UIPA”) provides access to public information. Visit our Open Records Request page to submit a request.
Office Details
## Our Honolulu Office Details
Most of our offices are located in two main buildings downtown. Select a tab below to find the address and contact information
  * King Kalakaua Building
  * Leiopapa A Kamehameha Building
  * Kekuanao`a Building


##  OAHU 
King Kalakaua Building aka United States Post Office Custom House and Court House
  * 335 Merchant Street Honolulu, Hawaii 96813


﻿
!Room 100 tag Room 100 
### Office of Administrative Hearings 
Conducts hearings and issues recommended decisions for cases originating in the department. Administers the Medical Inquiry and Conciliation Panel and the Design Professional Claims Conciliation Panel
  * mail to   oah@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 9,%20press%20option%209)


!Room 101 tag Room 101 
### Cable Television Division 
Issues cable franchises, monitors service quality, handles complaints, and advocates for affordable broadband access
  * mail to   cabletv@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 8,%20press%20option%208)


!Room 201 tag Room 201 
### Business Action Center 
Provides general assistance with state business registration requirements.
  * mail to   bac@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  808-586-2545


!Room 201 tag Room 201 
### Business Registration Division 
Registration for corporations, partnerships, LLCs, trade names, trademarks and service marks
  * mail to   breg@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 2,%20press%20option%202)


!Room 205 tag Room 205 
### BREG-Office of the Securities Commissioner 
Regulates registration of securities, broker-dealers, sales agents, investment advisers, representatives, and franchises. Investigates and prosecutes securities fraud and state securities/franchise law violations. Provides free investor education materials, presentations, and displays
  * mail to   seb@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 2, then option 3,%20press%20option%202,%20then%20option%203)


!Room 213 tag Room 213 
### Insurance Division 
Oversees Hawaii’s insurance industry, licenses companies, examines fiscal health, reviews rates/policies, investigates complaints, and regulates/promotes captive insurance
  * mail to   insurance@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 4,%20press%20option%204)


!Room 221 tag Room 221 
### Financial Institutions 
Regulates all state-chartered and state licensed financial institutions, escrow depositories, money transmitters, mortgage servicers, mortgage loan originators, and mortgage loan originator companies through various programs of licensing, enforcement and examinations
  * mail to   dfi@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * mail to   dfi-nmls@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 6,%20press%20option%206)


!Room 301  tag Room 301 
### Professional and Vocational Licensing Division (PVL) 
Oversees 25 boards/commissions and 24 programs for professional licensing and regulation
  * mail to   pvl@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 1,%20press%20option%201)


!Room 310 tag Room 310 
### Director’s Office 
Sets policies and provides leadership for the department and oversees the activities of the department
  * mail to   dcca@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 10,%20press%20option%2010)


!Room 326  tag Room 326 
### Consumer Advocacy Division 
Protects consumer interests in regulated utility and transportation services proceedings
  * mail to   dca@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  808-586-2800


!Room 333 tag Room 333 
### PVL-Real Estate Branch 
Provides guidance and educational materials on real estate issues and condominium governance
  * mail to   hirec@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press option 8,%20press%20option%208)


### Hawaii Post-Secondary Education Authorization Program (HPEAP) 
Authorization of accredited post-secondary degree-granting institutions
  * mail to   hpeap@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  808-586-7327


##  Leiopapa A Kamehameha Building 
  * aka State Office Tower, 235 South Beretania Street Honolulu, Hawaii 96813


﻿
!9th Floor tag 9th Floor 
### Consumer Resource Center 
File complaints against licensed professionals and report unlicensed activity. Get RICO Complaints History and check if a person has a professional and vocational license.
  * call to  1-844-808-3222


!9th Floor tag 9th Floor 
### Lemon Law Program 
State Certified Arbitration Program helps resolve disputes with auto manufacturers for defective vehicles and can help you get a refund
  * mail to   scap@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-3222


!9th Floor tag 9th Floor 
### Mortgage Foreclosure Dispute Resolution 
Owner-occupants of residential property in non-judicial foreclosure can meet with lenders to modify loans or work out payment plans within 3 months
  * mail to   MFDR@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  808-586-2886


!9th Floor tag 9th Floor 
### Regulated Industries Complaints Office 
Investigates and prosecutes complaints relating to licensed professionals and unlicensed activity 
  * mail to   rico@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  1-844-808-3222


!Room 801 tag Room 801 
### Consumer Protection Office 
Investigates consumer complaints about unfair trade practices, advertising, door-to-door sales, refunds, vehicle rentals, and credit practices
  * mail to   ocp@dcca.hawaii.gov
  * call to  808-586-2630 


!Room 801 tag Room 801 
### Landlord-Tenant Information Center 
Residential landlord-tenant questions answered Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM (except state holidays)
  * call to  808-586-2634


##  Kekuanao`a Building 
  * 465 South King Street, Room 103 Honolulu, Hawaii 96813


!Room 103 tag Room 103 
### Public Utilities Commission 
Regulates all chartered, franchised, certificated and registered public utility companies operating in the state.
  * call to  (808) 586-2020%20586-2020)

# Starting a Business in Hawaii
Step-by-step guidance, licensing information, and trusted state resources to help you research, register, and operate your business with confidence
## 1. Research the Market
Before launching your business, thorough market research is essential to understand demand, competition, and opportunities in Hawaii’s unique economy.
This step helps validate your idea, identify target customers, and gather data on industry trends, demographics, and economic indicators.
**Key Resources for Market Research**
## 2. Create a Business Plan
Banks often require business plans as part of their loan consideration process. These plans set forth the business’ goals, timelines, revenue projections, and other factors that can demonstrate the viability of your venture.
The following organizations offer free business counseling and assistance with drafting business plans:
  * Hawai’i Small Business Development Center Network


  * Service Corps of Retired Executives (SCORE)


You may also find sample business plans for various business activities on the
  * U.S. Small Business Administration’s website


## 3. Choose a Legal Structure
When starting a business, make an informed decision on how you will own and operate your business. Your choice of entity (business structure) can affect personal liability, management of the business, tax filing responsibilities, as well as estate planning considerations.
We suggest you seek additional advice from a qualified attorney or accountant before making a selection. Here, we provide general information for you to consider.
The most common types of business structures are:
  * Sole Proprietorship


  * General Partnership


  * Limited Partnership


  * Limited Liability Partnership


  * Limited Liability Limited Partnership


  * Corporation


  * S Corporation


  * Limited Liability Company


For more discussion on entity types, visit the following sites:
  * Hawaii Revised Statutes


  * From the SBA Website


## 4. Choosing a Business Name
After selecting a legal structure, you may register your business with the Department of Commerce & Consumer Affairs’ Business Registration Division (downloadable forms are available on their site).
To check if the business name you want is available (not already registered by another business), click on the “Search for a Business Name” link on this page (right bar).
For more information, visit our Fast Facts on Trade Names and Marks
## 5. Permits and Licenses
The Business Action Center provides information on the various licenses and permits associated with specific business activities in Hawaii. We can also give you the contact information of the issuing agency.
Call one of our offices or search the links below to view some of the most common areas of licensing and permitting.
**State of Hawaii Information**
State tax licenses: Not sure if your license is still active? Search for your tax license on the Department of Taxation’s website.
**Department of Agriculture**
  * Importing plants, animals, and microorganism to Hawaii
  * [Exporting plants and livestock](https://hdoa.hawaii.gov/pi/pq/export-program/)
  * Quality assurance
    * Commodities Branch
    * Measurement Standards


**Department of Commerce & Consumer Affairs**
  * Professional & Vocational Licensing Division
  * Real Estate Licensing Branch
  * Public Utilities Commission


Department of Health
Department of Transportation
**County Information**
Call one of our offices for information on special permits and licenses required by the four county governments.
You can also view their websites by clicking a county here:
  * Hawai’i
  * Honolulu
  * Kaua’i
  * Maui


## 6. Tax Info
Taxes are levied by federal, state, and local (county) governments. Follow the links below to learn more about the different tax types and systems. The Internal Revenue Service is our federal taxing authority, while state taxes are (with a few exceptions) administered by the Hawaii Department of Taxation. County governments in Hawaii administer our Real Property Tax.
  * Internal Revenue Service
  * [Apply for an Employer Identification Number](https://www.irs.gov/businesses/small-businesses-self-employed/employer-id-numbers)
  * [Tax Information for Businesses](https://www.irs.gov/businesses)


  * Small Business/Self-Employed
  * Hawai’i State Department of Taxation All persons doing business in Hawaii must obtain a General Excise Tax license. Contact us for more info on other tax licenses.
  * Honolulu County Real Property Tax
  * Maui County Real Property Tax
  * Kaua’i County Real Property Tax
  * Hawai’i County Real Property Tax


## 7. Employer Responsibilities
If you plan to hire employees, contact us on how to register with the Hawai’i Department of Labor and Industrial Relations.
Hawai’i State Department of Labor and Industrial Relations
  * Find the Employer’s Handbook here
  * Unemployment Insurance Division
  * Disability Insurance Division


_Hawaii law requires payment of the Unemployment Insurance Tax and may require insurance coverage for employees (e.g., Workers’ Compensation, Temporary Disability, and/or Prepaid Health Care). For more information go to the FAQs link on_ _DLIR’s_ _website._
  * HIOSH (Hawaii Occupational Safety & Health)
  * Federal U.S. Department of Labor


_Administers employment standards and practices such those pertaining to child labor, minimum wages, overtime, the Federal Medical Leave Act information (FMLA), and more._
  * Federal Occupational Health & Safety (OSHA)
  * Hawai’i State Department of Taxation


_Contact us if you need to register for state income tax withholding for employees_
  * Withholding Tax – For Employers

Forms, Fees and Information
# Registration
In Hawaii, corporations, partnerships, limited liability companies, limited liability partnerships, trade names, trademarks, service marks, and publicity name rights are registered with the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, Business Registration Division (BREG)
!A group of young adults laughing around a laptop in a office sitting
Business Information
## Register Your Business in Hawaii
You can [register online](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/BizEx/home.eb) to instantly submit a filing and receive a receipt. Or you can file using a paper form.
### Register Online
Many of the Business Registration Division’s forms are available for filing through our online portal, Hawaii Business Express (HBE).
list of forms available for online filing.
[Register Online](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/BizEx/home.eb)
### Fees
Please see the Business Registration Division’s [Form Fee Schedule](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Form-Fee-Schedule-12-2022.pdf) for fee details by entity.
Read more information on the State Archive preservation fee.
We accept Visa, MasterCard, Discover, American Express, Diners Club, or JCB. Use the [Business Registration Division Credit Card Transaction Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/BREG_Credit-Card-Transaction-Form_fillable.pdf), OR be sure to include the following information in order for payment to be processed: credit card number, type of card, name of cardholder, card expiration date, CVV code, amount of charge, indicate expedited or regular review, a contact telephone number and an email address.
### Registration Forms
All of the Business Registration Division’s forms, including those that are not yet available for online filing are available for download and printing in fillable PDF format. To submit a paper filing, select your business entity type (below), click on “Complete listing of all fillable PDF forms” and select a form to view, fill, and print.
**Print, sign, and include payment information** and submit to the Business Registration Division via email, mail, fax, or in person. Credit card information must accompany email or fax filings. For mail or walk-in filings, payment by check or credit card is accepted.
**Email registrations to:** breg-doci-filing@dcca.hawaii.gov
**Fax:** (808) 586-2733%20586-2733)
**Mailing address:**  
Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs  
Business Registration Division  
P.O. Box 40  
Honolulu, HI 96810  
**Street address:** 335 Merchant Street, Room 201, Honolulu, HI 96813
If you need assistance, please view our Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs), email breg@dcca.hawaii.gov, or call 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)), option 2.

Ensuring Fair Practice
# For Consumers and Public
We ensure public trust with high professional standards and clear regulation, providing clarity and resources to make confident choices, protect rights, and resolve issues.
!Women making a payment at a store checkout
  * File a Complaint
  * Hire a Licensed Contractor
  * Business Check
  * Lemon Law
  * [Landlord-Tenant Information](https://cca.hawaii.gov/consumers/#tenantinformation)
  * Consumer Notice


!Person typing on a laptop keyboard
Get Resolution
## File a Complaint
If you can't resolve an issue directly with a business or professional, filing a complaint is your next step. We handle a wide range of issues to protect consumers, including:
  * Unfair or deceptive business practices and professional misconduct
  * Disputes with financial, insurance, or investment services
  * Problems related to cable, public utilities, and other regulated services
  * Reports of individuals or companies operating without a required license

 Start the Complaint Process
Hire with Confidence
## How to Hire a Licensed Contractor?
The Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs emphasizes you take the time to do your research and hire a licensed contractor. We are providing you with 
  * Resources and Handouts
  * Tools to verify contractor licenses
  * List of Licensed Contractors
  * Information on how to report unlicensed activity

 Learn More
!Thumbnail !Play Button
!City scape at sun set
Research First
## BusinessCheck
BusinessCheck provides comprehensive background information on businesses and licensed professionals in Hawaii. Other resources include:
  * Licensee Name Search
  * Licensee Complaint History
  * Business Name Search
  * Business Complaint History
  * General Excise Tax License Search

 See More Options
!the sky is cloudy
Consumer Protection
## Lemon Law Information
Hawaii's State Certified Arbitration Program helps consumers resolve new motor vehicle warranty disputes. The program provides arbitration services for cars and motorcycles that cannot be properly repaired under manufacturer warranty. You will also find:
  * Consumer information
  * Manufacturer Information
  * Statistics by Year

 File for Arbitration
!a man holding a bunch of keys
Rental Guide
## Landlord-Tenant Information
The State's official resource for landlord-tenant matters. We provide the complete legal code, helpful handbooks, and important legislative updates and other resources such as:
  * Information Center Contact Details
  * FAQs
  * Directory of Agencies and Organizations

[ Access Landlord-Tenant Handbook](https://cca.hawaii.gov/landlord-tenant-information-center/)
!Person typing on a laptop keyboard
Threat Watch
## Consumer Notices
Stay ahead of scams. The Office of Consumer Protection posts timely alerts on fraud, data breaches, and emerging consumer threats in Hawaii, including:
  * The newest tactics scammers are using for rentals, gift cards, and phishing
  * Crucial warnings about security breaches that could impact your data
  * Enforcement actions taken against unlicensed or deceptive businesses

 See All Notices
!DCCA Hawaii logo

# Check The Status Of Your Application
## To Check the status of your Pending Application:
Visit –
**<https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/public-license-search/> **(Select “Pending Application”)
Please allow up to 7 business days for receipt and record of your status to appear on the web site
Application processing time is generally 45-60 business days, from receipt of a completed application.
**Processing time may be longer or delayed for reasons below:**
  * Application incomplete (missing information, fees, documents)
  * Pending receipt of 3rd party documents (education, experience, employment)
  * Pending Exam or Board/Program approval

* Pursuant to Act 108, Session Laws of Hawaii 2014, the administrative rules of the Public Utilities Commission remain in effect until amended, repealed, or overruled by the Public Utilities Commission. The administrative rules may be found on the Commission’s web site (<https://puc.hawaii.gov/about/statutes-rules-orders/>).

License Continuation Info
## Renewal Information
(Renew by September 30 of every even-numbered year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of October 1, 2026 to September 30, 2028
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
August 10, 2026 to September 30, 2026
  * ##### Renewal Hardcopy Form Mailout:
August 7, 2026
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date:
September 30, 2026


Renew Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Entity – active status -
$338.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Sole Proprietor – active status - 
$338.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) RME – active status - 
$208.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) ALL – inactive status - 
$160.00


  * All licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by September 30, every even-numbered year.


  * Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.

**Additional Renewal Requirements**  
---  
|  **TAX CLEARANCE REQUIREMENTS**  
for Active Contractor Entity and Sole Proprietors
  * Must have current and valid tax clearance with DOTAX
  * Tax clearance must be within 6 months of renewing license
  * Tax clearance DOTAX name must match the contractor’s PVL license name

  
---  
**If DOTAX Name Does Not Match PVL Licensee Name** If you want to update your tax clearance name with the Hawaii Department of Taxation: 1. Sign into Hawaii Tax Online at: <https://hitax.hawaii.gov/> 2. Select More… tab, Registration tile, select Manage Names & DBAs. 3. Navigate to the tax account, select the Name you would like to update. 4. Complete the request and select Submit.” OR Please contact the Department of Taxation at 808-587-4242 between the hours of 7:45 AM – 4:00 PM (Hawaii Standard Time), Monday to Friday, except state holidays to obtain a valid tax clearance that matches your PVL license name exactly.  
Firms and Sole Proprietors
RMEs
Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information (Restore within 60 days after license expiration)
Forfeited licenses may be restored within 60 days and subject to meeting restoration requirements. Failure to restore a forfeited license within 60 days after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.
  * ##### How to Request Hardcopy Restoration Form
[Learn More ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/request-hardcopy-restoration-form/)


#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Entity – active status -
$388.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) Sole Proprietor – active status - 
$388.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) RME – active status - 
$258.00
  * ##### Contractor (CT) ALL – inactive status - 
$210.00

##  Contractors License Board Meeting Schedule 
Meeting agendas are posted on the State Calendar site at least six calendar days before the meeting.
Contractor Applicant List
The current applicant list will be posted not less than fourteen (14) days prior to each Board meeting date and will be removed one (1) day after the scheduled Board meeting is held.

## Service Highlights
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic1.svg)
### File an Annual Report
Submit annual reports for active business entities online 
File Report  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic2.svg)
### Business Search & Documents
Look up businesses and purchase official registration documents
Start Search  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic3.svg)
### Verify a Professional
Confirm the active status and standing of any professional license
Check a License  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic4.svg)
### Consumer Scam Alerts
Stay ahead of fraud with timely warnings and safety tips
See Alerts  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic5.svg)
### Certificate of Good Standing
Purchase official proof of business registration and compliance
[Order Now  ](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/documents/search.html?mobile=N&site_preference=normal)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic6.svg)
### Renew license
Keep credentials active by paying renewal fees online
Renew Now  
## Consumers and Public
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic7.svg)
### Utility and Cable Issues
Resolve disputes with utility providers and cable franchises
Get Utility Help  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic8.svg)
### Hire a Licensed Contractor
Tips and tools to verify a contractor or professional license online
Get Hiring Tips  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic9.svg)
### Business Check 
Research tools to verify businesses and their complaint history
View Tools and Resources  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic10.svg)
### Lemon Law
Is your new motor vehicle suffering from repeated problems, and is it under the manufacturers warranty?
See if You Qualify  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic11.svg)
### Landlord-Tenant Information
Consult the official handbook on renter and landlord rights
[View Handbook  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/landlord-tenant-information-center/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic12.svg)
### Consumer Notices
Latest warnings on local scams and data security breaches
See All Alerts  
## Businesses and Professionals
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/business1.webp)
### Start a Business
Register entities, reserve trade names, and apply for licenses

### Manage Compliance
Submit annual reports and verify continuing education status 
Manage Compliance Online  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/business3.webp)
### Regulated Industries
Is your current occupation in a regulated industry?
View Regulated Industries  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic16.svg)
### Institutions
Compliance mandates for regulated financial institutions
View Institution Resources  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/business5.webp)
### My PVL Portal
Renew licenses and update personal records via online portal
Go to My PVL

# Boards and Programs
Welcome to the official directory for Hawaii’s professional boards and licensing programs. Please select a category below to access specific licensing requirements, renewal information, and applications
## Quick Access
### List of Boards and Programs
Go to [ ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards-and-programs/#list)
### Contact Info
Go to [ ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards-and-programs/#contact)
LODE MORE
## Who Regulates These Boards and Programs

---|---

# Hire a Licensed Contractor
The Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs emphasizes you take the time to do your research and hire a licensed contractor. Please review the information below to learn more about the importance of licensed contractors.
### RICO’s Top 10 Tips
!Top 10 Tips for Construction Projects
#### **  
Resources and Handouts**
  * [RICO’s Top 10 Tips for Consumers](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-Top-10-Tips-for-Construction-Projects.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Information for Consumers About the Contractors Recovery Fund](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-CLB-CRF.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Hire a Licensed Contractor Because](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-Hire-a-Licensed-Contractor-1.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Risks and Responsibilities of Being an Owner Builder](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-CLB-Owner-Builder.pdf) (PDF)


**Is your contractor licensed?**
Check here: <https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/public-license-search/>
**Are you thinking about remodeling your home? As a homeowner, you’ll probably hire a contractor to do the work. Make sure you do your homework before you begin your project.**
Click on the following link to watch a public service announcement on the importance of hiring a licensed contractor:
#### When is a contractor’s license required?
A contractor’s license is required:
  * for any project exceeding $1,500 in labor and material
  * for any project that requires a building, electrical or plumbing permit.


Electrical and plumbing contractors must employ licensed electricians and plumbers to perform the actual electrical and plumbing work, respectively.
#### Why hire a licensed contractor?
  * A licensed contractor has the training and experience necessary to obtain a contractor’s license. The State of Hawaii’s Contractor’s License Board has checked into the contractor’s background and is satisfied that the person is qualified to hold a contractor’s license.
  * A licensed contractor has worker’s compensation and liability insurance. This protects you as a homeowner from putting your property at risk if anyone is injured on your project or if anything is damaged while the work is being done.
  * A licensed contractor is able to obtain and sign building permits.
  * Lastly, if anything goes wrong with your home improvement project, the Contractor’s Recovery Fund may be available to you only if you have hired a licensed contractor.


#### How can I be sure a contractor is licensed?
Get the contractor’s name and license number and call the Licensing and Business Registration Information Section at 808-587-4272 or click on the following link: www.businesscheck.hawaii.gov .
!Business check logo
#### How do I report unlicensed activity?
If you come across a person or business that is engaged in or offering to do contracting work but you discover that the person or business does not hold a contractor’s license, please notify the Regulated Industries Complaints Office at 808-587-4272 or call toll-free 1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press 3. In order for RICO to investigate unlicensed activity, it is important to provide as much information as possible.
This is the kind of information that would be important to provide, especially if the unlicensed person or company has already started to actively work at a jobsite:
  * Written contracts or quotes from the unlicensed person reflecting the work to be done and the amount to be paid, and canceled checks or receipts if any payments were made;
  * A physical description of each of the workers on the jobsite (gender, height, weight, ethnicity, age, phone number) and how many people are working; description of any vehicles on the jobsite including color, make, model and license number;
  * Type of work performed, exact address/location, when the work started and the approximate hours that work occurs, estimated time of completion, and any other relevant information.


To report on-going unlicensed activity, please complete a **Report of On-Going Unlicensed Activity**. Mail or fax completed forms to RICO.
  * [Report On-Going Unlicensed Activity](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RICO-Report-of-On-Going-Unlicensed-Activity-3-2019-rev.pdf) (PDF)


#### **To File a RICO Complaint**
If you have entered into a contract or agreement with an unlicensed company or individual, please complete a RICO Professional Licensing Complaint Form. Please be sure to review the Frequently Asked Questions on the File a RICO Complaint page
  * [Contractor Complaint Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RICO-COMPLAINT-FORM-CONTRACTOR-2-11-2019-rev.pdf) (PDF)


File a Tip or Complaint

# Open Records Request
The [Uniform Information Practices Act](https://oip.hawaii.gov/laws-rules-opinions/uipa/) (Modified), chapter 92F, Hawaii Revised Statutes (“UIPA”), is Hawaii’s public records law and enables increased citizen participation in state government through access to public information. For more information on the state’s open records law or submitting UIPA requests, please see the State of Hawaii’s Office of Information Practices frequently asked questions.
**Requests to Other State and County Agencies**
Records requests should be submitted to the appropriate state and county agencies. A listing and directory of state and county agencies is available below.
State of Hawaii Departments & Agencies
County Government Departments & Agencies
_• For County of Hawaii, County of Maui, County of Kauai, and City and County of Honolulu_
**For Requests to the Hawaii Department of Commerce & Consumer Affairs**
Open records requests to the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA) can be submitted by completing a [Request to Access a Government Record Form](https://oip.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2014/05/Request-Access-form-12.1.151.pdf) to the contacts below.
Requests not within the purview of DCCA should be addressed to the appropriate entity in the state and county directory listed above.
Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs  
335 Merchant Street, Room 310  
Honolulu, Hawaii 96813  
(808) 586-2850  
Email: dcca@dcca.hawaii.gov

Official Certificates
## Print Your License Pocket Id And Wall Certificate Online
Effective April 18, 2019, you must retrieve and print your pocket ID card and wall certificate via your MyPVL account. No other notice will be provided.
Log in with your existing MyPVL account or sign up for a new MyPVL account
Go to MyPVL [List of License Types Issued Wall Certificates](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Wall-Certificates-by-License-1-1.pdf)
!"A hand holding a blank blue card
Who We Are
## About Us
The Professional and Vocational Licensing Division (PVL) implements regulatory standards for 52 distinct professions and vocations, overseeing 25 boards and commissions alongside 27 specialized licensing programs. Our mission is to maintain a fair marketplace by ensuring the public has access to competent, licensed practitioners through efficient processing and rigorous consumer protection.
**Division Branches and Services**
**Licensing Branch :** The Applications and Records sections conduct intensive reviews of all initial filings to determine licensure eligibility. This branch manages the issuance of new licenses, processes renewals, and maintains the state’s comprehensive licensing database. Through this database, the public can immediately verify license status, original licensure dates, expiration dates, and any history of disciplinary sanctions.
**Examination Branch :** This branch ensures the integrity of the licensing process by arranging the administration of all examinations. Staff members work to confirm the validity and reliability of exams, advising boards on technical aspects to ensure testing remains a consistent measure of professional competency.
**Administration Branch** serves as the liaison between the director of the department and the 25 boards and commissions administratively attached to DCCA. On a daily basis, the Administration Branch handles board affairs such as the coordinating, preparing, facilitating and recording of board meetings; ensuring board compliance with applicable laws that guide and direct their conduct; and implementing board decisions on applications, policies and procedures, and disciplinary actions. The Administration Branch also responds to requests for interpretation of the licensing laws and rules; oversees licensing and examination activity; and guides and assists with regulatory compliance issues. The same activities are done for the 27 programs (absent the necessity of board meetings) on behalf of the director of the department. There are also substantial coordination and facilitation activities performed by the Administration Branch with other state, county, and federal agencies involved with licensing and regulatory issues.
**Real Estate Branch** serves as the liaison between the director of the department and the Real Estate Commission, and performs the same functions as the Administration Branch for real estate licensing and regulation, and condominium property regimes. With the largest volume of applicants, licensees, and registrations, the Branch administers the Real Estate Recovery Fund, Real Estate Education Trust Fund, and the Condominium Education Trust Fund. The Branch also administers education and research programs in the field of real estate and condominiums.
**In summary** , all Branches within PVL work toward efficient and expedient processing of applications, licenses, and renewals to ensure the public has choices amongst competent, licensed practitioners.
PVL continues to forge ahead with its initiatives to improve accessibility to licensing services and information, and to deregulate and streamline the licensing process without compromising consumer protection. PVL is committed to working with licensees and the public to achieve the optimum balance between thoughtful and fair regulation, and consumer protection. In these difficult economic times, PVL is mindful of the strain on Hawaii’s businesses and seeks to integrate licensing measures and procedures that will facilitate business. Ultimately, PVL’s goal is to achieve and facilitate a fair marketplace for the State of Hawaii.
View More

# Hawaii Residential Landlord-Tenant Information Center
The Office of Consumer Protection (OCP) operates a free service providing information on Hawaii’s Residential Landlord-Tenant Code
!buildings and trees are along the road
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/contact-us.svg)
### Contact Us
#### Hours :
Monday - Friday, 8:00 AM - 12:00 Noon
(Closed state holidays)
#### Response Time :
Calls before 12:00 Noon →   
Returned same day by 4:30 PM
Calls after 12:00 Noon →   
Returned next business day by 4:30 PM
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/phone-icon.svg)
### Office Numbers
  * #### Maui Office (Maui, Molokai & Lanai)
1-844-808-DCCA (3222))
  * #### Hilo Office (Hawaii Island)
1-844-808-DCCA (3222))
  * #### Honolulu Office (Oahu/Kauai)
1-844-808-DCCA (3222))


![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/resources.svg)
### Resources
  * Residential Landlord-Tenant Code 
  * Chapter 521 - Residential Landlord-Tenant Code (Hawaii Revised Statutes) 
  * [Residential Landlord-Tenant Handbook (PDF) ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/2024-Landlord-Tenant-Handbook-Final.pdf)


#### Important to Know
This service provides general information about Hawaii’s Residential Landlord-Tenant Code (HRS Chapter 521) only. The information provided does not constitute legal advice. For specific legal guidance, consult an attorney…
## Maui Eviction Moratorium Information And Resources
Act 278 is a two-year pilot program (February 5, 2026 – February 4, 2028) that requires landlords to participate in mediation if a tenant requests it within 10 days of receiving an eviction notice for nonpayment of rent.
  * [ACT_278 Program Overview](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/ACT_278_Program_Overview.pdf)
  * [Act_278 FAQS](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Act278FAQS.pdf)
  * [PEMP LANDLORD FLYER](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/PEMP_LANDLORD_FLYER.pdf)


## Act 202 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
NOTE: This is not legal advice. This is only a simplified summary of parts of Act 202. This does not cover or apply to all situations. You should seek legal advice if you are a landlord or a tenant.
[ ![pdf icon](https://dccadev.exemplifi.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/pdf-icon.webp) Download a PDF copy of the FAQs ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/FAQ-Act-202-Eviction-Moratorium-Expiration.pdf)
##  Does this mean tenants who haven’t paid their rent will be evicted immediately? 
No, tenants will not be evicted immediately. Act 202 allows tenants and landlords to resolve rent issues through mediation before going to court. Both landlords and tenants must follow the process required under ACT 202.
##  Understanding Act 202. 
Act 202, signed into law on July 5, 2024, provides tenants with the option of mediation to avoid eviction. Mediation is not automatically required but becomes a mandatory step only if the tenant requests mediation and schedules it within 15 days after receiving the landlord’s notice.
The mediation process brings landlords and tenants together in person or by Zoom with an impartial third party called a mediator. The mediator helps the landlord and tenant try to voluntarily reach an agreement about overdue rent and/or eviction. The mediator does not take sides or tell the parties what to do. If the parties reach an agreement, the mediator will put the terms of the agreement in writing. The mediation process is paid for by State funds and is FREE for both landlord and tenant.
The opportunity to schedule a mediation will start on Wednesday, February 5, 2025.
##  When will Act 202 take effect? 
Currently, landlords are not allowed to file lawsuits for evictions relating to wildfires, as the Governor has an eviction moratorium (ban) in place. The eviction ban will end on Tuesday, February 4, 2025. Act 202’s mediation process starts the next day – Wednesday, February 5, 2025.
##  Why was this law passed? 
Many tenants cannot pay their rent due to financial hardships or displacement caused by the wildfires. The law tries to balance the rights of landlords and tenants by helping them negotiate agreements with the help of a mediator and avoid the court process.
##  What is mediation? 
Mediation is a process where opposing parties can resolve conflicts without going to court. An impartial mediator helps the parties talk and negotiate agreements that work for them. The mediator does not tell the parties what to do. It is up to the parties whether they want to reach an agreement or not.
##  What is litigation? 
Litigation is the process or practice of settling a dispute in a court of law.
##  Mediation process 
**I am a tenant, when can I sign up with Maui Mediation Services if I know I am late paying my rent?**
You can sign up right away, even before receiving a notice from your landlord. If you know you are behind on your rent, we recommend signing up as soon as possible.
Please go to Contact Maui Mediation Services. At the very minimum, please enter the following information in the online form:
The tenant named on the lease’s contact information:
  * **First and last name** ;
  * **Email address** that the tenant has access to;
  * **Phone number** the tenant can be reached at; and


Landlord information: (Enter it in the Defendant/Respondent Information Section)
  * **First and last name;**
  * **Email address;**
  * **Phone number** the landlord can be reached at;
  * **Mailing address**


(Please complete as much of your landlord’s information as possible so that Maui Mediation Services can contact them to schedule a mediation appointment.)
  * **Case type**(this is located towards the bottom of the online form and is a drop-down list, the likely selection would be: “landlord/tenant”)
  * **Comment** (located at the very end of the form) – you can leave a comment with any additional information you think will be helpful for Maui Mediation Services.


##  Who is responsible for mediation costs? 
The mediation process is paid for by State funds and is FREE for both landlord and tenant.
The state has contracted Maui Mediation Services to process mediation requirements under Act 202.
  * Maui Mediation Services office is open by appointment only
  * Address: 95 Mahalani Street, Suite 25, Wailuku, Hawaiʻi , 96793
  * Maui Mediation Services (MMS)
  * Email: landlordtenanthelp@mauimediation.org


Phone: 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)) (8:30 – 4:30 PM)
For community-based legal resources, please visit Maui Recovers’ Legal Resources.
##  What is the Act 202 process? 
Under Act 202, a landlord must provide the tenant with a written notice 15 days before filing for an eviction in court. The landlord must serve the tenants with a Notice, as well as send a copy to Maui Mediation Services.
Once the tenant receives the landlord’s Notice, it is important to be proactive and schedule a mediation session with Maui Mediation Services within 15 days (the tenant has an additional 2 days if the landlord mailed the Notice). The mediation session itself does not have to occur within the 15 days—it just must be scheduled. The landlord is required to wait 30 days from the date of the Notice before filing the eviction case in court. This 30 day period allows time for mediation to occur and for both parties to potentially reach an agreement.
Once an eviction lawsuit is filed in court, it generally takes 6-8 weeks before the court would issue an eviction order. It usually takes longer if a trial is requested.
##  What happens if a tenant does not respond to the notice? 
If the tenant fails to respond or schedule mediation within the 15-day notice period or does not show up to their scheduled mediation, the landlord may proceed with filing for eviction immediately.
##  What are the conditions under which a landlord can evict their tenant? 
Initially, when the moratorium ends, Landlords cannot start the eviction process unless the tenant is four months or more behind on their rent and provides the tenant with a notice. The following month, landlords may provide a notice to tenants who are three months or more behind. The third month, landlords may provide a notice to tenants who are two months or more behind. And then finally, in the fourth month, landlords may provide a notice to tenants who are one month or more behind. This phased approach helps manage the workload at Maui Mediation Services and gives tenants more time to pay their overdue rent before legal action is taken.
The table below outlines the time periods, corresponding start and end dates, and due and overdue rent amounts required for landlords to begin legal action, based on the expiration of the eviction moratorium on February 4, 2025.
**Phases of initiating evictions based on the moratorium expiration date** | **Start Date** | **End Date** | **Overdue Amount**  
---|---|---|---  
Day 1 to Day 30 | February 5, 2025 | March 6, 2025 | Equal to or greater  
than **four months of rent**  
Day 31 to Day 91 | March 7, 2025 | May 6, 2025 | Equal to or greater than **three months of**  
**rent**  
Day 92 to Day152 | May 7, 2025 | July 6, 2025 | Equal to or greater  
than **two months of rent**  
Day 153 to Day 365 | July 7, 2025 | February 4, 2026 | Equal to or greater  
than **one month of****rent**  
##  Do tenants have to leave their homes by the date on the written notice? 
No, tenants do not have to leave their homes by the date specified in the written notice. Eviction can only occur after a judge issues an order permitting the landlord to evict the tenant. Landlords generally cannot physically remove tenants, touch their personal property, change the locks, or shut off the utilities.
##  Service provider information: Maui mediation services Additional resources and assistance 
Service Provider: Maui Mediation Services
**Email:**landlordtenanthelp@mauimediation.org
**Social Media:**Maui Mediation Facebook
**Landlord-Tenant Mediation Phone :**1-844-808-DCCA (3222))
**Hours open:** 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM – Monday to Friday
Maui Mediation Services office is open by appointment only at:
95 Mahalani Street, Suite 25, Wailuku, Hawaiʻi 96793
Show More
## FAQs for Residential Landlords and Tenants on the Island of Maui
What you should know about your rights after the 20th Wildfires Emergency Proclamation, issued on February 4, 2025
[ ![pdf icon](https://dccadev.exemplifi.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/pdf-icon.webp) Current PDF copy of this FAQ ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/20th-EP-Rental-Restrictions-Basic-FAQs.pdf)
[ ![pdf icon](https://dccadev.exemplifi.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/pdf-icon.webp) Archived FAQs ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/ocp/archived-maui-landlord-tenant-faqs/)
##  When did the emergency restrictions relating to rent increases begin? 
Restrictions on rent increases began on August 9, 2023, when the Second Proclamation Relating to Wildfires was issued.
##  What are the restrictions on increasing rents on the Island of Maui during this emergency? 
For the duration of this Emergency Proclamation, landlords are prohibited from increasing rents during a tenancy unless the rent increases are contained in a written agreement that was signed before this emergency period. These pre-written rent increases may be given effect as agreed upon in writing. Landlords may pass on additional operating expenses to tenants and must be able to provide documentation for these expenses.
##  What are the restrictions on terminating existing residential tenancies on the Island of Maui? 
There are no restrictions, however, landlords and tenants must follow the procedures outlined in Act 202.
##  What is an example of a material breach of a lease other than a breach for non-payment of rent? 
Failing to perform an obligation under the rental agreement or lease, which constitutes the consideration for entering into the contract, such as failure to follow obligations, rules, and restrictions set by the landlord, willfully destroying, or damaging the dwelling unit, or allowing another to willfully destroy or damage the dwelling unit, may be material breaches of the lease.
##  Do the emergency restrictions for rentals apply to commercial leases on the Island of Maui? 
These emergency restrictions apply only to residential leases and only on the island of Maui.
##  When do the restrictions end? 
The restrictions will continue until April 5, 2025, unless terminated or superseded earlier. The Governor may increase the duration of these restrictions by issuing another proclamation.
##  Where are these emergency restrictions in effect? 
These restrictions apply to residential leases on the entire island of Maui and are not limited to any specific location on Maui.
##  Is the Residential Landlord-Tenant Code in effect? Are courts hearing landlord-tenant cases during this emergency period? 
The Residential Landlord-Tenant Code (chapter 521, HRS) remains in effect throughout the State for the duration of this Emergency Proclamation, with two exceptions. For the Island of Maui only, certain laws are suspended.
##  Does a displaced Maui resident residing in a hotel, motel, or a condominium operated as a residence for persons displaced by the wildfire emergency have the same rights as a tenant under the Residential Landlord-Tenant Code (chapter 521, HRS)? 
No. For the duration of this Emergency Proclamation, hotels, motels, and condominiums are not considered landlords even if they temporarily house non-congregate shelterees. Similarly, persons displaced by the wildfire emergency who are temporarily housed in hotels, motels, and condos (i.e., non-congregate shelterees) are not considered tenants under state law for the duration of this Emergency Proclamation.
##  Can a person displaced by the wildfire emergency who is currently and temporarily living in a Red Cross-arranged condominium unit continue to live in the unit beyond the time limit contained in the condominium by-laws, declarations, and house rules? 
Yes. Condo owners are relieved from strict compliance with certain provisions of the governing documents that may limit the amount of time a unit can be rented.
These FAQs constitute guidance from the Office of Consumer Protection. This guidance is non-binding. The Office of Consumer Protection is not your attorney and cannot provide you with legal advice

    * Request License Change
    * [Wall Certificate List](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Wall-Certificates-by-License-2.pdf)
  *  Reports
  *  FAQs


# Request for a License Verification
## Request for a License Verification
Verification of licenses can be done on-line at no cost using our license search site at <https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/public-license-search/>.
You or the requesting agency will be able to obtain information such as original license date, license expiration date, license status, disciplinary action, etc.
The information downloaded from this site is official license information which state boards or other individuals can download for themselves.
If the above is not acceptable, please submit a written request.
**Request must include:**
  * The license name and license number
  * The complete name and mailing address that the verification is to be mailed to
  * Check for $15 license verification fee (check payable to Commerce and Consumer Affairs)


**Mail Request To:**
Licensing Branch-License Verification
P.O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801
**Please allow twenty (20) business days from receipt of your request for PVL standard verification reports to be mailed out.**

Trust. Fairness. Growth.
# Protecting Consumers, Supporting Business
We have organized our services to create a clear path for consumers, businesses and institutions.
!Hero Image
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/themes/dcca/dcca-hawaii/src/assets/images/white-quote.svg)
## Director’s Message
Aloha and welcome to the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs’ (DCCA) website. We hope you will take a moment to learn about the various services we offer for individuals and businesses in the State of Hawaii.
Our mission at the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs is to protect Hawaii’s consumers and service its business community with respect and fairness to the interests of both.
The hardworking staff of the DCCA serve the public through a variety of functions, including regulating and licensing more than 140,000 professionals, monitoring the financial solvency of local banks and insurance companies, and investigating complaints of fraudulent and unfair business practices.
!Nadine Ando Image
## Nadine Ando
Director, DCCA
!Dean Hazama Image
## Dean Hazama
Deputy Director, DCCA
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/themes/dcca/dcca-hawaii/src/assets/images/white-quote.svg)
## Deputy Director's Message
Aloha! Thank you for visiting the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs’ (DCCA) website. At DCCA, we are committed to helping individuals and businesses navigate commerce in our state with confidence and trust. Guided by service and accountability, our team provides dependable services, clear direction, and responsive support to our communities across the islands.
Whether you are a consumer seeking information, a professional maintaining a license, or a business operating in Hawaii, we hope you find this website useful and easy to navigate. We also encourage you to use our feedback features to help us make your future experiences better. Mahalo for allowing us to serve you and the people of Hawaii.
We’re Here to Help.
##  Have questions? Contact our office or visit us in person. 
Mahalo nui loa,
Nadine Ando
Director, Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs
  * 335 Merchant Street, Honolulu, HI 96813
  * (808) 586-2850%20586-2850)


Clear Paths. Better Service.
## Divisions
We have organized our services to create a clear path for consumers, businesses, and institutions
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-19.svg)
### Business Registration Division (BREG)
(BREG)Registers and maintains records for all business entities in Hawai
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-2.svg)
### Cable Television Division (CATV)
Regulates cable companies and helps resolve subscriber complaints
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-20.svg)
### Division of Consumer Advocacy (DCA)
Represents consumer interests in public utility and transportation cases
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-3.svg)
### Division of Financial Institutions (DFI)
Oversees state-chartered banks, credit unions, and financial service providers
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-7.svg)
### Insurance Division (INS) 
Regulates cable companies and helps resolve subscriber complaints
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-21.svg)
### Office of Administrative Hearings (OAH)
Conducts hearings and issues disciplinary decisions for all DCCA divisions
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-22.svg)
### Office of Consumer Protection (OCP)
Investigates unfair business practices and landlord-tenant issues
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-23.svg)
### Professional and Vocational Licensing (PVL)
Licenses and regulates over 50 different professions and vocations
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-24.svg)
### Regulated Industries Complaints Office (RICO)
Investigates complaints against licensed professionals and unlicensed activity
Go to  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Mask-group-10.svg)

## Career Opportunities
Join us for dynamic opportunities that support consumer advocacy and business services, and build a rewarding career in Hawaii’s public sector   
  
All current job openings are posted on GovernmentJobs.com
  * Account Clerk V 
  * Administrative Assistant I-II 
  * Financial Institution Examiner I 
  * Financial Institution Examiner II
  * Insurance Examiner II

 View All Open Positions

## Service Highlights
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic1.svg)
### File an Annual Report
Submit annual reports for active business entities online 
File Report  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic2.svg)
### Business Search & Documents
Look up businesses and purchase official registration documents
Start Search  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic3.svg)
### Verify a Professional
Confirm the active status and standing of any professional license
Check a License  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic4.svg)
### Consumer Scam Alerts
Stay ahead of fraud with timely warnings and safety tips
See Alerts  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic5.svg)
### Certificate of Good Standing
Purchase official proof of business registration and compliance
[Order Now  ](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/documents/search.html?mobile=N&site_preference=normal)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic6.svg)
### Renew license
Keep credentials active by paying renewal fees online
Renew Now

Need Help?
## Guidance for Filing a Complaint
Resources and instructions to help you file your complaint
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-1.svg)
### Report a PV&L Licensee or Unlicensed Activity in a PV&L Industry
Report issues with licensed professionals or unlicensed activities
 Go to Report a PV&L Licensee or Unlicensed Activity in a PV&L Industry  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-2.svg)
### Cable Provider
For complaints against cable television operators and companies
[ Go to Cable Provider  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/catv_complaint.pdf)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-3.svg)
### Financial Institutions
Banking, lending, and financial service issues
 Go to Financial Institutions  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-5.svg)
### Utility
Complaints about services like electricity, gas, water, and telecommunications
 Go to Utility  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-4.svg)
### Insurance Entity
Complaints against insurance companies, agents or coverage practices
 Go to Insurance Entity  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-6.svg)
### Securities Entity
Securities and franchise law violation complaints
[ Go to Securities Entity  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/sec/complaint-form-and-instructions/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-14.svg)
### General Commerce
Unfair or deceptive trade or commerce practice complaints
 Go to General Commerce  
!Illustration showing customer service and support resources
## How Can We Help You?
##  Check a License or Complaint History 
Find out if a contractor is licensed or view a business’s prior complaints
##  File a Complaint 
Many DCCA divisions offer complaint forms online, accessible through their home pages
##  Learn About Hawaii's Lemon Law 
Get information about the law and the arbitration process
##  Get Landlord-Tenant Information 
Access resources and information regarding the Landlord-Tenant code
##  Find Business Information 
Look up details about registered businesses and know who the officers are for a corporation
### Contact Our Support Center
Centralized toll free number 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)).
Know Your Rights
## Consumer and Public Resources
A complete library of official consumer guides, and information to help you make confident consumer decisions
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-29.svg)
### Scam Protection
Common scams and practical steps for protecting yourself from fraud
 Go to Scam Protection  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-30.svg)
### Foreclosure Information Center
Struggling with Your Mortgage? Don’t Wait. Explore Options to Prevent Foreclosure
[ Go to Foreclosure Information Center  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/hfic/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-31.svg)
### Landlord-Tenant Code
The official Code, plus handbooks and materials for renters and property owners
[ Go to Landlord-Tenant Code  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/landlord-tenant-information-center/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-32.svg)
### FAQs
Answers to FAQs regarding our services, and consumer rights
 Go to FAQs  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-33.svg)
### Alerts
Latest consumer alerts, public warnings, and important news issued by the state
 Go to Alerts  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-19.svg)
### Condo Information
Publications for condominium owners, buyers, and boards of directors
[ Go to Condo Information  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/reb/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-34.svg)
### Investors
Information on securities registration, investor education, and filing complaints
 Go to Investors  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-35.svg)
### Financial Education
Guides for your financial education, plus resources on consumer protection
 Go to Financial Education  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/mega-menu-ic3.svg)
### Purchase Lists of PVL Licenses
Details on requesting and purchasing official lists of active licensees
 Go to Purchase Lists of PVL Licenses

## Business Resource
Everything your business needs for research and compliance, including forms, reports, data tools and guides

Gateway to Hawaii’s open data portal for public datasets and business intelligence

### BREG Reports
Official reports and statistical data published by the Business Registration Division
 Go to BREG Reports  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-37.svg)
### Business Data
Tool for creating and purchasing lists of business registration data
 Go to Business Data  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-6.svg)
### List Builder Securities
Information on securities registration, compliance, and regulatory statutes
 Go to List Builder Securities  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-38.svg)
### Publications and Official Guides
Library of subdivision registration applications, reports, and forms
[ Go to Publications and Official Guides  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/programs/subdivision/application-forms-publications/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-39.svg)
### State Data Portal
Gateway to Hawaii’s open data portal for public datasets and business intelligence
 Go to State Data Portal  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-40.svg)
### Real Estate Licensees
Details on license management, continuing education, complaint records, and more
 Go to Real Estate Licensees  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-30.svg)
### Prelicensing Information
Resources for candidates seeking to meet pre-licensing education standards
[ Go to Prelicensing Information  ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/reb/general-prelicensing-information-and-prelicense-school-resources/)
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-32.svg)
### FAQs
Answers to common questions about the business registration
 Go to FAQs  
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-41.svg)
### Utility Glossary
Glossary of utility industry terms and acronyms used in regulatory proceedings
 Go to Utility Glossary

# Contractors License Board Examination
## Examinations
Effective January 2023, the contractor examinations will be administered by PSI Services, LLC.
Applications must be approved by the Board before applicants will be allowed to register for the examination.
Upon Board approval to take the examination(s), applicants will receive an examination approval letter which will indicate the examination(s) they are eligible to take. The letter will also include information on how to register for the examination directly with PSI and the date that the examination(s) must be passed by. Please allow up to 24 hours after the issuance of the approval letter to register for your examination.
Please note that applicants must pass the examination(s) within six (6) months. Applicants who do not pass the examinations within six (6) months must re-apply by filing a new application.
For more information regarding an examination, including registration, see the candidate bulletin at <https://test-takers.psiexams.com/hitrade>. Alternatively, you may register via telephone at (833) 333-4754.

# Contractors License Board Application Forms and Publications
### CONTRACTORS LICENSE BOARD APPLICATION FILING DEADLINE
### EFFECTIVE DECEMBER 2020  
---  
Application, fee and all supporting documents to be presented to the Board **MUST** be received in the Board’s office **on or before the first Tuesday of the month TWO MONTHS prior to the scheduled meeting date.**  
### [***VIEW APPLICATION DEADLINE AND EXAMINATION DATES***](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/contractor/application-deadline-examination-dates/)  
---  
### ONE YEAR (1) ABANDONMENT OF APPLICATIONS
Pursuant to HRS §436B-9 Your application shall be considered abandoned, will be destroyed, if you fail to complete the license process within one year after filing the application, **or fail to take and pass the examination within 6 months after becoming eligible to take the examination.** If an application is deemed abandoned the applicant shall be required to reapply for licensure and comply with the licensing requirements at the time of the reapplication.

## How Can We Help You?
##  Check a License or Complaint History 
Find out if a contractor is licensed or view a business’s prior complaints
##  File a Complaint 
Many DCCA divisions offer complaint forms online, accessible through their home pages
##  Learn About Hawaii's Lemon Law 
Get information about the law and the arbitration process
##  Get Landlord-Tenant Information 
Access resources and information regarding the Landlord-Tenant code
##  Find Business Information 
Look up details about registered businesses and know who the officers are for a corporation
### Contact Our Support Center
Centralized toll free number 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)).
Know Your Rights
## Consumer and Public Resources
A complete library of official consumer guides, and information to help you make confident consumer decisions
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/icon-29.svg)
### Scam Protection

# Business Registration Division (BREG)
BREG oversees the registration of business entities and the regulation of the securities industry in Hawaii. We maintain the public business registry, process filings for corporations and LLCs, and enforce state laws to protect investors
Quick Access
