## Beauty School Renewal Information
(Renew by December 31 of every year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2027
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
November 3, 2025 to December 31, 2025
  * ##### Renewal Hardcopy Form Mail Out:
October 31, 2025
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date:
December 31, 2025


#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Beauty School (BSC) - active status – 
$380.00
  * ##### Beauty School (BSC) - inactive status –
$12.00


  * All Licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by December 31 of every year.
  * Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.


License Continuation Info
## Barber, Beauty Operator, Shop and Instructor Renewal Information
(Renew by December 31, of every odd-numbered year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2026 (Must renew by hardcopy only)
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
November 3, 2025 to December 31, 2025
  * ##### Renewal Hardcopy Form Mail Out:
October 31, 2025
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date:
December 31, 2025


Renew Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Barber (BAR) – active status - 
$146.00
  * ##### Barber (BAR) – inactive status - 
$12.00
  * ##### Barber Shop (BAS) - active status - 
$176.00
  * ##### Barber Shop (BAS) - inactive status - 
$12.00
  * ##### Beauty Operator (BEO) – active status - 
$146.00
  * ##### Beauty Operator (BEO) – inactive status - 
$12.00
  * ##### Beauty Shop (BSH) - active status -
$176.00
  * ##### Beauty Shop (BSH) - inactive status - 
$12.00
  * ##### Beauty Instructor (I) - active status – 
$146.00
  * ##### Beauty Instructor (I) - inactive status – 
$12.00


  * All Licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by December 31 of every odd-numbered year.


  * Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.


Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information (Restore within 3 years after license expiration)
(BAR, BEO, BAS, BSH, I) Forfeited licenses may be restored within 3 years and subject to meeting restoration requirements.  
Failure to restore a forfeited license within 3 years after the license expiration date will require a written explanation along with supporting documentation and will be subject to approval  
by the board.
  * ##### Restore Online (BAR only): BEO, BAS, BSH and I must restore by hardcopy only
https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov 


#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Barber (BAR) – active status 
$196.00
  * ##### Barber (BAR) – inactive status
$62.00
  * ##### Barber Shop (BAS) - active status
$276.00
  * ##### Barber Shop (BAS) - inactive status
$112.00
  * ##### Beauty Operator (BEO) – active status
$171.00
  * ##### Beauty Operator (BEO) – inactive status
$37.00
  * ##### Beauty Shop (BSH) - active status
$276.00
  * ##### Beauty Shop (BSH) - inactive status
$112.00
  * ##### Beauty Instructor (I) - active status 
$171.00
  * ##### Beauty Instructor (I) - inactive status
$37.00


## Regulating Body
The official Board of Barbering and Cosmetology, together with its schedule of public meetings and the minutes from past proceedings

Michael Basta Jr., Beauty Operator
Ashley Cornelio, Barber

Vacant, Public
Vacant, Public
**Qualifications**  
2 Barbers  
2 Beauty Operators  
3 Public
##  Board of Barbering and Cosmetology Meeting Schedule 
Meeting agendas are posted on the State Calendar site at least six calendar days before the meeting.
**2026 Board Meeting Schedule**  
---

# Pest Control Operator Firm and Sole Proprietor Renewal Requirements
**PEST CONTROL OPERATOR RENEWAL REQUIREMENTS (for the licensing period of July 1, 2020 to June 30, 2022)**  
---  
**FIRMs and SOLE PROPRIETORs** (Active Status) Must be current with the below at the time of renewal:
**Liability insurance** , valid thru July 1, 2020  
---  
**Workers compensation insurance** , valid thru July 1, 2020 (If no employees-must have filed exclusion from chapter 386, HRS form)  
**Tax clearance or tax compliance letter from the Hawaii Department of Taxation,** no older than 6 months of July 1, 2020  
**Designated Principal RME**  _**(All RMEs must also be renewed before license will be renewed.)**_  
**SOLE PROPRIETOR and RMEs** (Active Status) **Must submit proof of valid, current certification** with the Department of Agriculture in the appropriate category and subcategories (section 16-94-40, HAR) with your renewal.

# Board of Nursing
The Board licenses Certified Public Accountants, administers exams, enforces standards, renews licenses, and conducts disciplinary actions ensuring ethical accounting practices
Licensing Made Simple
## Your License Essentials
Access our online license management tool, read the latest announcements, and find answers to your questions
### MyPVL - Manage Your PVL License Online
Go to  
### Nursing Programs Information
Go to [ ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/hawaii-nursing-programs/)
LODE MORE
License Options
## Nursing License Types
  * Licensed Practical Nurse
  * Registered Nurse
  * Advanced Practice Registered Nurse


Licensing Assistance
## Applicant and Licensee Information
Find application forms, requirements, deadlines, laws, as well as guides on how to manage your existing license online
[Remedial/Refresher Course Offering at University of Hawaii @ Hilo](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Nursing-Remedial-Refresher-Course-Info-23.pdf)
### Application and Licensing Information
  * [Application Forms, Requirements, Fees and Instructions](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/nursing/application_publications/)
  * [Application Deadlines and Examination Dates](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/nursing/application-deadline-examination-dates/)

  * Licensing Laws and Rules (HRS/HAR)

### How To
  * [Check the Status of Your Application](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/check-the-status-of-your-application/)
  * Inactivate or Reactivate Your License
  * Request an Address or Name Change or Duplicate License (pocket ID/wall certificate)
  * Request a License Verification
  * Manage Your License Online With Your MyPVL LICENSEE DASHBOARD


License Continuation Info
## Renewal Information 
(Renew by June 30 every odd-numbered year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of July 1, 2025 to June 30, 2027
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
April 21, 2025 to June 30, 2025
  * ##### Renewal Postcard Mailout:
April 18, 2025
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date:
June 30, 2025


Review Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) – active status
$196.00
  * ##### Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) – inactive status
$12.00
  * ##### Registered Nurse (RN) – active status
$196.00
  * ##### Registered Nurse (RN) – inactive status
$12.00
  * ##### Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN) - active status
$36.00
  * ##### Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN) - inactive status
$12.00


[2025 RENEWAL REQUIREMENTS & FAQs (Continuing Competency & Criminal Background History Check)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/2025-NURSING-RENEWAL-REQUIREMENTS-AND-FAQS-final.pdf)
  * All Licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by June 30 of every odd-numbered year.
  * Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.


Additional Renewal Requirements
  * Criminal History Record Check
  * Continuing Competency Requirement
  * [APRN Requirement](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards/nursing/advanced-practice-registered-nurse-aprn-renewal-requirements/)


Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information (Restore within 2 years for LPN and RN; 6 months for APRN after license expiration)
(LPN and RN) Forfeited licenses may be restored within 2 years and subject to meeting restoration requirements. Failure to restore a forfeited license within 2 years after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.
(APRN) Forfeited licenses may be restored within 6 months and subject to meeting restoration requirements. Failure to restore a forfeited license within 6 months after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.
NOTE: ALL Restoration and reactivation applicants are required to comply with the electronic fingerprinting requirement for a criminal history/background check and provide proof of continuing competency prior to the restoration or reactivation of the license.
#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) – active status
$226.00
  * ##### Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN) – inactive status
$42.00
  * ##### Registered Nurse (RN) – active status
$226.00
  * ##### Registered Nurse (RN) – inactive status
$42.00
  * ##### Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN) - active status
$66.00
  * ##### Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN) - inactive status
$42.00


## Continuing Competency Information
2023 RESTORATION REQUIREMENTS & FAQs (Continuing Competency & Criminal Background History Check)
##  Continuing Competency Renewal Requirements 
**Beginning on July 1, 2017, all Hawaii nurse licensees** who do not meet one of the exemptions will be required to complete one of the learning activity options for continuing competency prior to the renewal of his/her Hawaii nurse license in 2019. Please review the [Continuing Competency Booklet](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/files/2013/06/BON-CCG-INFO-2017.pdf).
**The board may conduct a random audit** to determine whether the continuing competency requirements have been met.
**Continuing Competency – Preceptor Forms**
[Preceptor Form for Nursing Employees (fillable form)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/files/2019/02/ContinuingCompetencyForms_PreceptorHoursForEMPLOYEES_V5_Final_Fillable.pdf)
[Preceptor Form for Nursing Students (fillable form)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/files/2019/02/ContinuingCompetencyForms_PreceptorHoursForSTUDENTS_V5_Final_Fillable.pdf)

# How to Request: Address/Name Change/Pocket ID/Wall Certificate
## Print Your License Pocket Id and Wall Certificate Online
Effective April 18, 2019, you must retrieve and print your pocket ID card and wall certificate via your MyPVL account. No other notice will be provided.
**Go To:** https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov
[List of License Types Issued Wall Certificates](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Wall-Certificates-by-License.pdf)
## Address Changes Accepted Online, Beginning November 4, 2019
**Go To:** https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov  
---  
[List of Licenses that may submit online address changes](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/ONLINE-ADDRESS-CHANGES-ALLOWED-11-19.pdf)  
(License Status MUST be ** _“Current, Valid & In Good Standing”_**)  
**Processing time for online address changes: 3-5 business days**  
## Hardcopy Address/Name Change Forms
  * Submit address/name change form only if you cannot submit address changes online;
  * Submit address/name change form if you have a name change;
  * Select address change form applicable for your license type and follow instructions contained in form;
  * Incomplete requests will not be processed;
  * Your records will not be updated if your license is not current, valid and in good standing;

  
---  
**Processing time for hardcopy address/name change requests:****20-30 business days from receipt of request form**  
[HEALTH CARE PROFESSIONALS – Address/Name Change Request Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Address-Name-Change-Request-Health-Care-Prof-0605R.pdf)  
(Physician, osteopathic Physician, Physician Assistant, Podiatrist, Emergency Medical Technicians, Naturopathic Physician and Nurse)  
[REAL ESTATE PROFESSIONALS – Address/Name Change Request Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CF_Updated_2023.pdf)  
(Real Estate Broker, Real Estate Salesperson, Condominium Management Association, Condominium Hotel Operator)  
[ALL OTHER LICENSE TYPES – Address/Name Change Request Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Address-Name-Change-Request-0925.pdf)

# Manage Your License Online With Your MyPVL Licensee Dashboard
**What is MyPVL?**
MyPVL is a home for all PVL licensees to access information and services 24/7 from a single dashboard.
  * View your license information (status, insurance, class, employees etc…)
  * Print your license pocket ID card/Wall Certificates
  * Renew/Restore your license online (For online eligible license types) 
    * Update your address with your online renewal
    * Print a copy of your online renewal


**How do I access MyPVL?**
“Sign up” or “login” with a myHawaii account at mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov.

## Release: RELEASE: CINDY MATSUSHITA NAMED STATE’S LICENSING ADMINISTRATOR
Share 
**FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**  
April 15, 2025
HONOLULU — Cindy Matsushita has been named the Licensing Administrator for the state of Hawai‘i Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA) Professional and Vocation Licensing Division (PVL), effective April 14. As Licensing Administrator, Matsushita is responsible for the regulatory oversight of 52 different professions and vocations within the state.
Twenty-five (25) licensing regulatory boards and commissions are administratively attached to PVL, as well as twenty-seven (27) licensing programs (those without a board or commission). The division provides staff support to the licensing regulatory boards, handles applications, licenses, reviews and processes renewals, and maintains license records. The division also provides guidance for proper implementation of the licensing laws and administrative rules for the 52 licensing areas. PVL is divided into four branches, which are the Licensing Branch, Examination Branch, Administration Branch, and Real Estate Branch.
Matsushita has spent her 25-year public sector career in various legal and administrative roles, performing legal defense work at the Tri-County Metropolitan Transportation District of Oregon, as the Board Executive Officer for the Honolulu Authority for Rapid Transportation Board of Directors, and as the Student Transportation Services Administrator for the Hawai‘i State Department of Education.
“Licensing is more than a regulatory function—it’s a bridge between qualified professionals and the communities they serve,” said Matsushita. “I look forward to the continued enhancement of our processes and strengthening trust in Hawai‘i’s professional landscape,” continued Matsushita.
“The Licensing Administrator plays a vital role in ensuring that professionals across Hawai‘i meet the standards our communities expect and deserve,” said DCCA Director Nadine Ando. “Cindy Matsushita’s dedication to fair and transparent service will be a great asset to our team and to our community.”
Matsushita is a graduate of Punahou School, the University of Hawai‘i at Manoa, and Lewis & Clark Law School.
The Professional and Vocational Licensing Division continues to forge ahead with initiatives to improve accessibility to licensing services and information and streamline the licensing process without compromising consumer protection. PVL is committed to working with licensees and the public to achieve the balance between thoughtful and fair regulation, and consumer protection. In these difficult economic times, PVL is mindful of the strain on Hawai‘i’s businesses and seeks to integrate licensing measures and procedures that will facilitate a fair marketplace for the state of Hawai‘i.

# Athletic Trainers Program
Overview of Athletic Trainer credentialing, documentation requirements, renewals, and regulatory obligations for practitioners in Hawaiʻi.
Licensing Made Simple
## Your License Essentials
Access our online license management tool, read the latest announcements, and find answers to your questions
### MyPVL- Manage Your License
Go to  
LODE MORE
License Options
## Athletic Trainers Registration Types
Athletic Trainers
Licensing Assistance
## Licensing Resources
Find application forms, requirements, deadlines, laws, as well as guides on how to manage your existing license online
### Application and Licensing Information
  * [Application Forms, Requirements, Fees and Instructions](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/programs/athletictrainer/application_publication/)
  * [Licensing Laws and Rules (HRS/HAR) ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/programs/athletictrainer/statute_rules/)
  * [Abandonment of Application](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/1yrAbandonmentApplication-1-17.pdf)


### How To
  * [Check the Status of Your Application](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/check-the-status-of-your-application/)
  * Inactivate or Reactivate Your License
  * Request an Address or Name Change or Duplicate License (pocket ID/wall certificate)
  * Request a License Verification
  * Manage Your License Online With Your MyPVL LICENSEE DASHBOARD


License Continuation Info
## Renewal Information
(Renew by June 30 every 3 years (2025, 2028, 2031)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of July 1, 2025 to June 30, 2028 (Must Renew by Hardcopy Only)
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
May 5, 2025 to June 30, 2025
  * ##### Hardcopy Renewal Form Mailout
May 2, 2025
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date
June 30, 2025


Review Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Athletic Trainers (AT) – active status
$225.00
  * ##### Athletic Trainers (AT)– inactive status
$12.00


All licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal triennial by June 30, every three (3) years, (2025, 2028, 2031).
Licenses not renewed by license expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.
Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information  
(Restore within 1 year after license expiration)
**Forfeited licenses may be restored within 1 year and subject to meeting restoration requirements (Must restore by hardcopy only).**
  * ##### Failure to restore a forfeited license within 1 year after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.


#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Athletic Trainers (AT) – active status
$325.00
  * ##### Athletic Trainers (AT) – inactive status
112.00


Regulatory Entity Details
## Regulating Body
Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs – Director

# Travel Agency Program
Registration, financial security, and compliance requirements for travel agencies and sellers of travel in Hawaiʻi to protect consumers and ensure industry accountability
## Important Update!
All licenses must be renewed by December 31, 2023. See information below on renewing online. Hard-copy renewals may be requested by contacting the Licensing Branch at (808) 586-3000 or by emailing travel@dcca.hawaii.gov.
If your license was previously approved for a waiver of the client trust account requirement, do NOT answer “yes” or check the box for the first three attestations regarding the client trust account. Instead, you must submit a HARD COPY renewal with a signed letter that confirms the travel agency still:
  * Is not physically located in Hawaii;
  * Does not accept cash or check payments from its consumers to conduct its travel services/business;
  * All consumer payments for travel services arranged by the travel agency will only be made by electronic payment methods, such as credit charge or debit card; and
  * All payments received will be made directly to the travel providers/facilitator.


You may answer “yes” or check the box for the last attestation, “I am in compliance with all the provisions of Chapter 468L, HRS, regarding the client trust account” as applicable.
Licensing Made Simple
## Your License Essentials
### MyPVL- Manage Your License
Go to  
LODE MORE

  * Charter Tour Operator

  * [Licensing Laws and Rules (HRS/HAR)](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/programs/travel/statute_rules/)

License Continuation Info
## Renewal Information
(Renew by December 31 every odd-numbered year)
#### On-time renewals for the licensing period of January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2027
  * ##### Renewals Accepted:
November 3, 2025 to December 31, 2025
  * ##### Renewal Postcard Mail Out:
October 31, 2025
  * ##### Renew By License Expiration Date: 
December 31, 2025


Review Online
#### On-Time Cost to Renew
  * ##### Travel Agency (TAR) – active status
$138.00
  * ##### Travel Agency (TAR) – inactive status
$12.00


All Licenses regardless of issuance date, are subject to renewal by December 31 of every odd-numbered year.
Licenses not renewed by the expiration date are unlicensed and may not practice.
Regain Active Status
## Restoration Information  
(Restore within 1 year after license expiration)
Forfeited licenses may be restored within 6 months and subject to meeting restoration requirements.  
(Must restore by hardcopy only)  
Failure to restore a forfeited license within 1 year after the license expiration date will result in the termination of the license and the person must apply as a new applicant and meet current licensing requirements.
#### Cost to Restore (late renewal)
  * ##### Travel Agency (TAR) – active status
$158.00
  * ##### Travel Agency (TAR) – inactive status
$32.00

REB Overview
# Real Estate Branch (REB)
The Real Estate Branch, as part of the Professional and Vocational Licensing Division, assists the Real Estate Commission in carrying out its responsibility for the education, licensure and discipline of real estate licensees; registration of condominium projects, condominium associations, condominium managing agents, and condominium hotel operators; and intervening in court cases involving the real estate recovery fund.
For information regarding other boards and commissions, including real estate appraisers, subdivisions, and timeshares, please visit the Professional and Vocational Licensing Division website.
!Waikiki beech and lagoon with hotels and mountains

Who We Are
## About the Commission
The Hawaiʻi Real Estate Commission is composed of nine members appointed by the Governor and confirmed by the Senate, representing both licensed real estate professionals and the public from across the State of Hawaiʻi.
Administratively attached to the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs through the Professional and Vocational Licensing Division, the Commission oversees the licensure, education, and discipline of real estate professionals and the regulation of condominium-related entities to protect consumers and uphold the integrity of Hawaiʻi real estate industry.
For information regarding other boards and commissions, including real estate appraisers, subdivisions, and timeshares, please visit the Professional and Vocational Licensing Division website.
  * About the Commission
  * Meet the Commissioners
  * [Ex Parte Communication with Commissioners](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/04exparte.pdf)
  * Awards

# General Prelicensing Information
## General Information
##  General Information 
How to obtain and/or restore a real estate license, request for preliminary decision, change form, and other resource information
[Learn More](https://cca.hawaii.gov/reb/rec_forms/#accordion-item-1)
##  Prelicense School and Course Schedules 
  * **Prelicense School and Course Search**– Search by prelicense school, course dates, title, island, and/or medium (classroom or internet).


##  Salesperson Prelicense Candidate Information 
  * **[Steps for becoming licensed a Real Estate Salesperson](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/190605.salesflowchart.pdf)**
    * **[Request for Preliminary Decision](https://cca.hawaii.gov/reb/rec_forms/licensing)**  
Do you have a questionable background issue? Questionable background issues include, but is not limited to, criminal convictions (felony, misdemeanor, or court martial), or any civil/administrative/government action, against you, including DUIs, drug/alcohol issues, abuse situations, tax liens, unpaid tax obligations, judgments, unpaid judgments, disciplinary action (for another licensing area or from another state or country), applicants currently serving parole or probation, violations of administrative laws/court orders/government sanctions, etc. **If you have a questionable background issue** , the Commission highly recommends you review the attached application.
    * **Prelicense School and Course Schedules**
    * **Create a MyPVL Account**  
A MyPVL account is necessary when signing up for a prelicense course. Information provided in your account is often used to deliver the course completion certificate, which is required when sitting for the real estate examination. MyPVL is also a new home for all PVL licensees to access information and services 24/7 from a single dashboard. To access MyPVL, “Sign Up” for a free eHawaii.gov Account to get started or “Log In” with your existing eHawaii.gov account.
    * **[Salesperson Prelicense Curriculum](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/salesperson_curriculum_july_2005.pdf)**
      * [Anatomy of a Preliminary Title Report](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/anatomy_of_a_preliminary_title_report_-_final.pdf)
      * [Condominium Handout](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/condominium_handout_-_final.pdf)
      * [Directory of Real Estate Designations](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/directory_of_real_estate_designations_-_final.pdf)
      * [HARPTA / FIRPTA Handout](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/harpta_firpta_handout_-_final.pdf)
      * [Mortgage Terminology Defined](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/mortgages_terminology_defined_-_final.pdf)
      * [Property Management Basics](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/property_management_basics_handout_-_final.pdf)  
NOTE: The information provided in the handouts should only be used to familiarize individuals with some of the terms and requirements of the respective laws and should not replace the advice and counsel of a licensed tax adviser or attorney or the guidance of a Principal Broker or Broker-in-Charge who is responsible for the engagement.
    * **License Examination**
      * Examination Prerequisites
      * [Description of Examination](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/347hawaii-re-cib.pdf)
      * [Prelicense Examination Application Forms](https://cca.hawaii.gov/reb/rec_forms/#ExaminationApplicationForms)
        * Equivalency to the Prelicense Education Requirement: 
          1. [Instructions and Information for Prelicensing Education Equivalency – REAL ESTATE](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/EWINSTRUCT_161220.pdf)
          2. Application for Prelicensing Education Equivalency – REAL ESTATE  
([Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/EW_170601b.pdf "Application for Prelicensing Education Equivalency - Form Fillable")) ([Non-Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/EW_170601b_nonfill.pdf "Application for Prelicensing Education Equivalency - Non Form Fillable"))
          3. Verification of License – REAL ESTATE ([Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VOL05_2016.pdf "Verification of License - REAL ESTATE - Form Fillable")) ([Non-Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VOL05_2016nonfill.pdf "Verification of License - REAL ESTATE - Non Form Fillable"))
        * Equivalency to the Uniform Section of the Examination: 
          1. [Instructions and Information for Uniform Section of the Examination](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/UEEINSTRUCT_161010.pdf)
          2. Application for Equivalency to the Uniform Section of the Examination ([Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/UEE_190605.pdf "Application for Equivalency to the Uniform Section of the Examination")) ([Non-Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/UEE_NONFILL_190605.pdf "Application for Equivalency to the Uniform Section of the Examination - Non Form Fillable\)"))
          3. Verification of License – REAL ESTATE ([Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VOL05_2016.pdf "Verification of License - REAL ESTATE - Form Fillable")) ([Non-Fillable PDF](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VOL05_2016nonfill.pdf "Verification of License - REAL ESTATE - Non Form Fillable"))
    * **Exam Administrator – PSI Services, LLC**
PSI administers the Hawaii real estate licensing examination. Candidates may register for the real estate examination, download the Candidate Handbook, which includes the test content outline, and obtain other examination information on the PSI web site
      * PSI’s Holiday Schedule  
Memorial Day Closed  
Independence Day Closed  
Labor Day Closed September  
Thanksgiving Day Closed  
Christmas Closed  
New Years Closed
    * **New Licensee Frequently Asked Questions**


NOTE: The **Salesperson** license application is provided to candidates upon successfully passing the Hawaii real estate salesperson or broker examination at the test site. Successful examination candidates may also obtain the application by calling, writing, or faxing the **Real Estate Branch** .
##  Broker Prelicense Candidate Information 
  * **[Steps for becoming a licensed Real Estate Broker](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/190605.brokerflowchart.pdf)**
    * [Request for Preliminary Decision](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/PRELIM05_2016nonfill.pdf)  
Do you have a questionable background issue? Questionable background issues include, but is not limited to, criminal convictions (felony, misdemeanor, or court martial), or any civil/administrative/government action, against you, including DUIs, drug/alcohol issues, abuse situations, tax liens, unpaid tax obligations, judgments, unpaid judgments, disciplinary action (for another licensing area or from another state or country), applicants currently serving parole or probation, violations of administrative laws/court orders/government sanctions, etc. **If you have a questionable background issue** , the Commission highly recommends you review the attached application.
    * **Prelicense School and Course Schedules**
    * **Create a MyPVL Account**  
A MyPVL account is necessary when signing up for a prelicense course. Information provided in your account is often used to deliver the course completion certificate, which is required when sitting for the real estate examination. MyPVL is also a new home for all PVL licensees to access information and services 24/7 from a single dashboard. To access MyPVL, “Sign Up” for a free eHawaii.gov Account to get started or “Log In” with your existing eHawaii.gov account.
    * **[Broker’s Prelicense Curriculum](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/broker_curriculum.pdf)**

        * Broker Experience Certificate – **NOTE: Candidates for the broker examination are required to apply and be granted an “Experience Certificate” prior to registering for the examination with the testing company. Failure to obtain the Experience Certificate prior to examination will result in the invalidation of the examination score pursuant to Hawaii Administrative Rules, Section 16-99-19.2**
          1. [Instructions and Information for Broker Experience Certificate](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/BEINSTRUCT2_161010.pdf)

    * **Exam Administrator – PSI Services, LLC**  
PSI administers the Hawaii real estate licensing examination. Candidates may register for the real estate examination, download the Candidate Handbook, which includes the test content outline, and obtain other examination information on the PSI web site
      * PSI’s Holiday Schedule  
Memorial Day Closed  
Independence Day Closed  
Labor Day Closed September  
Thanksgiving Day Closed  
Christmas Closed  
New Years Closed
    * **New Licensee Frequently Asked Questions**


NOTE: The **Broker** (Individual) license application is provided to candidates upon successfully passing the Hawaii real estate salesperson or broker examination at the test site. Successful examination candidates may also obtain the application by calling, writing, or faxing the **Real Estate Branch** .

# File an Unlicensed Activity Tip or a Formal Complaint
**IMPORTANT** : Before submitting a complaint or tip, please visit RICO’s homepage and scroll down for educational resources, complaints history, lemon law matters, and information about the regulated industries that could affect your decision to file a tip or complaint.
Unlicensed Activity Tip  
[Formal Complaint](https://web2.dcca.hawaii.gov/ricocomplaint/)  
[Self-Help Letter](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Sample-Self-Help-Letter.pdf)
Many disputes and concerns are resolved through “self-help” even before a formal complaint is ever filed with RICO. Please use this letter to communicate your concerns before a complaint is filed.

# Hire a Licensed Contractor
The Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs emphasizes you take the time to do your research and hire a licensed contractor. Please review the information below to learn more about the importance of licensed contractors.
### RICO’s Top 10 Tips
!Top 10 Tips for Construction Projects
#### **  
Resources and Handouts**
  * [RICO’s Top 10 Tips for Consumers](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-Top-10-Tips-for-Construction-Projects.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Information for Consumers About the Contractors Recovery Fund](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-CLB-CRF.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Hire a Licensed Contractor Because](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-Hire-a-Licensed-Contractor-1.pdf) (PDF)
  * [Risks and Responsibilities of Being an Owner Builder](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/CLB-190718-CLB-Owner-Builder.pdf) (PDF)


**Is your contractor licensed?**
Check here: <https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/public-license-search/>
**Are you thinking about remodeling your home? As a homeowner, you’ll probably hire a contractor to do the work. Make sure you do your homework before you begin your project.**
Click on the following link to watch a public service announcement on the importance of hiring a licensed contractor:
#### When is a contractor’s license required?
A contractor’s license is required:
  * for any project exceeding $1,500 in labor and material
  * for any project that requires a building, electrical or plumbing permit.


Electrical and plumbing contractors must employ licensed electricians and plumbers to perform the actual electrical and plumbing work, respectively.
#### Why hire a licensed contractor?
  * A licensed contractor has the training and experience necessary to obtain a contractor’s license. The State of Hawaii’s Contractor’s License Board has checked into the contractor’s background and is satisfied that the person is qualified to hold a contractor’s license.
  * A licensed contractor has worker’s compensation and liability insurance. This protects you as a homeowner from putting your property at risk if anyone is injured on your project or if anything is damaged while the work is being done.
  * A licensed contractor is able to obtain and sign building permits.
  * Lastly, if anything goes wrong with your home improvement project, the Contractor’s Recovery Fund may be available to you only if you have hired a licensed contractor.


#### How can I be sure a contractor is licensed?
Get the contractor’s name and license number and call the Licensing and Business Registration Information Section at 808-587-4272 or click on the following link: www.businesscheck.hawaii.gov .
!Business check logo
#### How do I report unlicensed activity?
If you come across a person or business that is engaged in or offering to do contracting work but you discover that the person or business does not hold a contractor’s license, please notify the Regulated Industries Complaints Office at 808-587-4272 or call toll-free 1-844-808-DCCA (3222), press 3. In order for RICO to investigate unlicensed activity, it is important to provide as much information as possible.
This is the kind of information that would be important to provide, especially if the unlicensed person or company has already started to actively work at a jobsite:
  * Written contracts or quotes from the unlicensed person reflecting the work to be done and the amount to be paid, and canceled checks or receipts if any payments were made;
  * A physical description of each of the workers on the jobsite (gender, height, weight, ethnicity, age, phone number) and how many people are working; description of any vehicles on the jobsite including color, make, model and license number;
  * Type of work performed, exact address/location, when the work started and the approximate hours that work occurs, estimated time of completion, and any other relevant information.


To report on-going unlicensed activity, please complete a **Report of On-Going Unlicensed Activity**. Mail or fax completed forms to RICO.
  * [Report On-Going Unlicensed Activity](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RICO-Report-of-On-Going-Unlicensed-Activity-3-2019-rev.pdf) (PDF)


#### **To File a RICO Complaint**
If you have entered into a contract or agreement with an unlicensed company or individual, please complete a RICO Professional Licensing Complaint Form. Please be sure to review the Frequently Asked Questions on the File a RICO Complaint page
  * [Contractor Complaint Form](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RICO-COMPLAINT-FORM-CONTRACTOR-2-11-2019-rev.pdf) (PDF)


File a Tip or Complaint

# Hawaii’s Lemon Law
!Scap car logo
The RICO division administers Hawaii’s Lemon Law, which is more formally known as the State Certified Arbitration Program or SCAP. Hawaii’s Lemon Law helps consumers who have problems getting their new motor vehicle (cars and motorcycles) repaired under a manufacturer’s warranty. Through the program, a consumer may be awarded a refund or a replacement if their motor vehicle is determined to be a “lemon”.
[Consumer Information](https://web2.dcca.hawaii.gov/scap/)
[Manufacturer Information](https://cca.hawaii.gov/rico/manufacturer-information/)
Statistics by year
For more specific information,  
please call the RICO Consumer Resource Center at  
1-844-808-3222 or email scap@dcca.hawaii.gov.
State Certified Arbitration Program  
Regulated Industries Complaints Office  
235 S. Beretania Street, Ninth Floor  
Honolulu, Hawaii 96813  
Phone: (808) 586-2660  
Fax: (808) 586-2670

# The Regulated Industries
**What Industries, professionals, and tradespersons fall within RICO’s enforcement authority?**
In the interest of public health, welfare and safety, a professional or vocational license is required to perform certain services and work, in a regulated industry, for consumers. The professional or vocational license is not the same as a general excise tax license or business registration. The professional and vocational licenses that RICO has enforcement authority over are managed by the Professional and Vocational Licensing Division (PVL) of the DCCA, and governed by the industry board, commission or program.
A professional or vocational license is a significant and unique achievement for the holder. It cannot be loaned out to, or borrowed, by someone else. The license assures the consumer that the industry person they will be hiring has the minimum level of education, training and experience needed to competently perform the specialized work.

# Securities Enforcement Branch (SEC)
Protecting Hawaii’s financial landscape through rigorous securities enforcement, industry compliance oversight, and proactive investor education programs to ensure a fair and transparent marketplace
Quick Access
## Essential Resources and Tools
Access professional tools for securities compliance, enforcement actions, and investor education to ensure your firm meets all state regulatory requirements
### Securities Online Portal
Go to  
### Broker-Dealers
Go to  
### Complaint Form and Instructions
Go to [ ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/sec/complaint-form-and-instructions/)
### Enforcement Actions and Orders
Go to  
LODE MORE
Overview
## About the Securities Branches
The Securities Branches provide regulatory oversight over the securities industry and franchises in the State and are organized as follows:
  * **The Securities Compliance Branch** is responsible for the registration of securities broker-dealers, securities agents, investment advisers, investment adviser representatives, investment companies, securities offerings, and the filing of franchise offering circulars in the State. The branch also conducts field examinations of broker-dealers and investment advisers in Hawaii.
  * **The Investor Education Program (IEP)** , which falls under the Securities Compliance Branch, provides investor education and financial literacy outreach statewide. The IEP maintains outreach programs for kupuna (seniors) and caregivers, working families and union members, keiki (youths), industry professionals, military, and Hawaiian and other ethnic communities. The IEP is also the state coordinator of LifeSmarts, a national consumer education program for youth.


![People reviewing documents and using laptops during a work meeting](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/registration.webp)

Reach Out
## Contact Securities Branch
Securities staff remain available during normal business hours (7:45 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Monday – Friday, except state holidays) to meet the needs of the public over the phone, online, and in-person. For in-person services, indoor mask wearing is encouraged throughout DCCA’s offices.
Securities Enforcement: To file a complaint, please go to <https://hi-dcca.my.site.com/securities/s/> and submit the Securities Enforcement Branch Complaint Form through our online portal. If you have any questions or need assistance, please email seb@dcca.hawaii.gov or call 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)), option 2, then option 3.
Connect with us on Linktree at <https://linktr.ee/HISecurities> for links to all of our social media accounts.
The DCCA will be open 7:45 AM – 4:30 PM (Hawaii Standard Time), Monday to Friday, except state holidays.

# Complaint Form and Instructions
![](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/board1.webp)
## Submit a Complaint
The Securities Branches are responsible for regulating securities activities and franchising activities in the State of Hawaii. We oversee the firms and individuals who offer or sell securities, offer investment advice and offer or sell franchises to the public.
  * Via Securities Online Portal
  * [PDF (downloadable/fillable/printable)](http://files.hawaii.gov/dcca/sec/complaints/seb_complaint_form.pdf)


Before filling out the Complaint Form, please review these instructions. They will help you to understand our functions and better enable us to act on your complaint.
##  What we can do 
We investigate complaints against individuals and businesses for possible violations of state securities and franchise laws. We can take legal action against violators, and in appropriate cases, refer matters to other agencies for criminal prosecution. Please help us stop securities and franchise fraud by reporting fraud to us.
##  What we cannot do 
We cannot give legal or financial advice or act as your attorney.
In some instances, restitution is made to investors as a result of this agency’s actions. But often times investors will need to sue in civil court as a private matter in order to get their money back. You should consult an attorney for private legal matters. We cannot put people in prison: we do not have criminal jurisdiction.
##  How you can help us 
  * Organize your complaint using these guidelines:  
Tell us HOW you first learned of the investment opportunity or service.  
Tell us WHAT happened. Start from the beginning. Be specific as to what was said and who said it.  
Tell us WHO was present during these conversations or acts.  
Tell us WHEN and WHERE these conversations/acts took place.  
Tell us WHEN and WHERE the money and agreements changed hands.  
Tell us HOW you know the representations were false or HOW you know your money was misused.  
Tell us WHY you decided to take advantage of the investment opportunity or service.  
List the names, addresses and telephone numbers of any others who you believe may have similar complaints.
  * Enclose copies of documents such as stock certificates, bonds, limited partnership agreements, contracts, prospectuses, confirmations, letters, cancelled checks (front and back), advertisements, paper, notes, and any other documents that may explain your complaint. Do not send originals; we cannot be responsible for their safekeeping.
  * If you have any questions concerning the complaint form, you may call the Securities Enforcement Branch in Oahu at 1-844-808-DCCA (3222)), press 2, then press 3, or on the neighbor islands, between 7:45 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. for reporting fraud.
  * Upon completion of the form, please mail the complaint form along with all copies of any supporting documents to:Securities Enforcement Branch  
Business Registration Division  
Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs  
P. O. Box 40  
Honolulu, Hawaii 96810


Show More

## Our Services
We have organized our services to create a clear path for consumers, businesses, and institutions
### For the Public   
and Consumers
Consumer advocacy hub for ensuring a fair, and transparent marketplace
![a woman using a touch screen laptop](https://cca.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/ourservice1.webp)
  * [Verify a Professional's License](https://mypvl.dcca.hawaii.gov/public-license-search/)
  * [Search Disciplinary Actions and Judgments](https://cca.hawaii.gov/oah/release-dcca-disciplinary-actions-through-november-2025/)
  * [Report Unlicensed Activity in the PV&L areas](https://web2.dcca.hawaii.gov/tip/)
  * [Hawai‘i Lemon Law for Vehicles](https://cca.hawaii.gov/rico/lemon-law/)


[View all Services For the Public   
and Consumers ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/consumers/)
### For Businesses, Professionals and Institutions

  * [Apply for or Renew a Professional License](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl)

  * [File an Annual Report](https://hbe.ehawaii.gov/annuals/)
  * [Find Your Board’s Rules and Requirements](https://cca.hawaii.gov/pvl/boards-and-programs/)


[View all Services For Businesses, Professionals and Institutions ](https://cca.hawaii.gov/business/)
